cards.back: A partitional clustering algorithm that divides data into K clusters based on the mean distance of the features

cards.front: Density-based Spatial Clustering of Applications with Noise (DBSCAN)
cards.back: A density-based clustering algorithm that groups data points into clusters based on their density and proximity to each other
A clustering approach that divides the data space into a grid of cells and assigns data points to the cell with the closest center
Parameters used in DBSCAN algorithm to determine the density of the points and the minimum number of points required to form a dense region
A variation of the K-Means algorithm that initializes the centroids by selecting the K data points that are farthest from the current centroids
